---
title: 3-Node Cluster (with Auth)
description: Production-style 3-node Kafka KRaft cluster with SASL/PLAIN authentication.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

**Image:** `confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.8.0` (3 containers)  
**Ports:** `9092–9097` (external + controller), `29092–29094` (internal)  
**Data:** `kafka1_data`, `kafka2_data`, `kafka3_data` (named volumes)  
**Networks:** `kafka-net` (internal), `kafka-ui-net` (external, shared)

## Introduction

A 3-node Apache Kafka cluster running in KRaft mode (no ZooKeeper) with SASL/PLAIN authentication on all client-facing listeners. Designed for team environments and pre-production workloads where restricting broker access to known users is required.

## When to Use

- Multiple applications or team members connect to the same Kafka cluster
- The host is accessible beyond a single developer's machine
- You need to enforce per-client credentials for auditability
- You're working towards a production-grade Kafka setup

:::danger[Development use of no-auth cluster]
If you only need Kafka for local development on your own machine, use the [cluster without auth](/templates/kafka/cluster-without-auth/) instead. SASL setup adds complexity that isn't worth it for solo local development.
:::

## Architecture

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph TB
    subgraph Cluster["3-node KRaft Cluster (SASL/PLAIN)"]
        subgraph B1["kafka1"]
            SASL_EXT1["SASL_EXTERNAL\n:9092"]
            PLAIN_INT1["PLAINTEXT_INTERNAL\n:29092"]
            CTRL1["CONTROLLER\n:9093"]
        end
        subgraph B2["kafka2"]
            SASL_EXT2["SASL_EXTERNAL\n:9094"]
            PLAIN_INT2["PLAINTEXT_INTERNAL\n:29093"]
            CTRL2["CONTROLLER\n:9095"]
        end
        subgraph B3["kafka3"]
            SASL_EXT3["SASL_EXTERNAL\n:9096"]
            PLAIN_INT3["PLAINTEXT_INTERNAL\n:29094"]
            CTRL3["CONTROLLER\n:9097"]
        end
    end
    CTRL1 <-->|"KRaft quorum"| CTRL2
    CTRL2 <-->|"KRaft quorum"| CTRL3
    CTRL1 <-->|"KRaft quorum"| CTRL3

    ExtClient["External clients\n(SASL credentials required)"] -->|"KAFKA_HOST_IP:9092/9094/9096"| Cluster
    KafkaUI["Kafka UI\n(kafka-ui-net)"] -->|"kafka1-3:29092-29094"| Cluster
`} />

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine 20.10+, Docker Compose v2
- `kafka-ui-net` network: `docker network create kafka-ui-net`
- `KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID` and `KAFKA_HOST_IP` must be set (see below)

## Ports

| Port | Broker | Listener | Used by |
|---|---|---|---|
| `9092` | 1 | SASL_EXTERNAL | External clients |
| `9093` | 1 | CONTROLLER | KRaft quorum |
| `9094` | 2 | SASL_EXTERNAL | External clients |
| `9095` | 2 | CONTROLLER | KRaft quorum |
| `9096` | 3 | SASL_EXTERNAL | External clients |
| `9097` | 3 | CONTROLLER | KRaft quorum |
| `29092` | 1 | PLAINTEXT_INTERNAL | Kafka UI via kafka-ui-net |
| `29093` | 2 | PLAINTEXT_INTERNAL | Kafka UI via kafka-ui-net |
| `29094` | 3 | PLAINTEXT_INTERNAL | Kafka UI via kafka-ui-net |

## Configuration

```bash
cd kafka/confluent/3-node-cluster-with-auth
cp .env.example .env
```

**Required variables (no defaults):**

| Variable | How to get the value |
|---|---|
| `KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID` | `docker run --rm confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.8.0 kafka-storage random-uuid` |
| `KAFKA_HOST_IP` | See [Prerequisites](/prerequisites/#kafka-finding-your-host-ip) |
| `KAFKA_ADMIN_PASSWORD` | Choose a strong password (e.g., 20+ chars) |
| `KAFKA_TEST_PASSWORD` | Choose a strong password for the `test` client user |

**Optional port overrides:**

| Variable | Default |
|---|---|
| `KAFKA1_EXTERNAL_PORT` | `9092` |
| `KAFKA1_CONTROLLER_PORT` | `9093` |
| `KAFKA1_INTERNAL_PORT` | `29092` |
| `KAFKA2_EXTERNAL_PORT` | `9094` |
| `KAFKA2_CONTROLLER_PORT` | `9095` |
| `KAFKA2_INTERNAL_PORT` | `29093` |
| `KAFKA3_EXTERNAL_PORT` | `9096` |
| `KAFKA3_CONTROLLER_PORT` | `9097` |
| `KAFKA3_INTERNAL_PORT` | `29094` |

## Workflow

```bash
# Generate required values
docker run --rm confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.8.0 kafka-storage random-uuid
# Copy the output as KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID

# Find host IP (Linux)
ip route get 1 | awk '{print $7; exit}'

# Fill in .env
vim .env

# Start the cluster
docker compose up -d

# Verify all 3 brokers are running
docker compose ps

# Check broker logs
docker compose logs kafka1

# Stop
docker compose down

# Stop and wipe all topic data
docker compose down -v
```

## Connecting External Clients (SASL/PLAIN)

Bootstrap servers:
```
KAFKA_HOST_IP:9092,KAFKA_HOST_IP:9094,KAFKA_HOST_IP:9096
```

SASL configuration (Java properties):
```properties
security.protocol=SASL_PLAINTEXT
sasl.mechanism=PLAIN
sasl.jaas.config=org.apache.kafka.common.security.plain.PlainLoginModule required \
  username="admin" \
  password="your-admin-password";
```

Python (kafka-python):
```python
from kafka import KafkaProducer
producer = KafkaProducer(
    bootstrap_servers=['host:9092', 'host:9094', 'host:9096'],
    security_protocol='SASL_PLAINTEXT',
    sasl_mechanism='PLAIN',
    sasl_plain_username='admin',
    sasl_plain_password='your-admin-password',
)
```

## Connecting Kafka UI

Bootstrap servers for Kafka UI (internal, via kafka-ui-net):
```
kafka1:29092,kafka2:29093,kafka3:29094
```

Authentication: SASL/PLAIN, username `admin`, password from `KAFKA_ADMIN_PASSWORD`.

Note: Internal listeners use `PLAINTEXT` (no SASL) because the `kafka-ui-net` is a private Docker network. Only external listeners require SASL.

## References

- [Confluent Platform Kafka documentation](https://docs.confluent.io/platform/current/kafka/overview.html)
- [KRaft overview](https://developer.confluent.io/learn/kraft/)
- [SASL/PLAIN configuration](https://docs.confluent.io/platform/current/kafka/authentication_sasl/authentication_sasl_plain.html)
